methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rapidus is a late-stage semiconductor company that has raised $5.0B to date. The peer universe in Chips & Semiconductors comprises 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched. Valuation triangulation is constrained by limited same-stage peer data and the absence of disclosed revenue.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable approach here. Using total capital raised of $5.0B as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $6.0B. Peer-median triangulation is not viable; while 14 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) contained the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because Rapidus does not publicly disclose revenue; the public semiconductor median of 25.3x P/S across the three tickers is therefore not actionable without internal revenue figures.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median point of $6.0B. This range reflects the stage-progression method alone. Key uncertainties center on the absence of comparable-company multiples due to thin peer overlap at this stage and valuation tier, and the lack of revenue disclosure that would anchor public-market benchmarking. Stress-testing against disclosed financials or board-level revenue guidance would be required to narrow this range materially.
